rawText:
From the moment I became involved in the creation of new technologies, their ethical dimensions have concerned me, but it was only in the autumn of 1998 that I became anxiously aware of how great are the dangers facing us in the 21st century. I can date the onset of my unease to the day I met Ray Kurzweil, the deservedly famous inventor of the first reading machine for the blind and many other amazing things.

Ray and I were both speakers at George Gilder's Telecosm conference, and I encountered him by chance in the bar of the hotel after both our sessions were over. I was sitting with John Searle, a Berkeley philosopher who studies consciousness. While we were talking, Ray approached and a conversation began, the subject of which haunts me to this day.

I had missed Ray's talk and the subsequent panel that Ray and John had been on, and they now picked right up where they'd left off, with Ray saying that the rate of improvement of technology was going to accelerate and that we were going to become robots or fuse with robots or something like that, and John countering that this couldn't happen, because the robots couldn't be conscious.

While I had heard such talk before, I had always felt sentient robots were in the realm of science fiction. But now, from someone I respected, I was hearing a strong argument that they were a near-term possibility. I was taken aback, especially given Ray's proven ability to imagine and create the future.

I already knew that new technologies like genetic engineering and nanotechnology were giving us the power to remake the world, but a realistic and imminent scenario for intelligent robots surprised me. It's easy to get jaded about such breakthroughs. We hear in the news almost every day of some kind of technological or scientific advance. Yet this was no ordinary prediction.

In the hotel bar, Ray gave me a partial preprint of his then-forthcoming book The Age of Spiritual Machines, which outlined a utopia he foresaw—one in which humans gained near immortality by becoming one with robotic technology. On reading it, my sense of unease only intensified; I felt sure he had to be understating the dangers, understating the probability of a bad outcome along this path.

overview.title: Why the Future Doesn't Need Us

sentences.original: From the moment I became involved in the creation of new technologies, their ethical dimensions have concerned me, but it was only in the autumn of 1998 that I became anxiously aware of how great are the dangers facing us in the 21st century.
sentences.translation: 새로운 기술의 창조에 관여하게 된 순간부터 그 윤리적 차원은 나를 걱정시켜 왔지만, 21세기에 우리가 직면한 위험이 얼마나 큰지를 불안하게 인식하게 된 것은 1998년 가을이 되어서였다.

grammarElements.text: From the moment I became involved in the creation of new technologies
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (시간 — from the moment)

grammarElements.text: but it was only in the autumn of 1998 that I became anxiously aware
grammarElements.label: 강조구문 (It was ... that)

grammarElements.text: of how great are the dangers facing us in the 21st century
grammarElements.label: 감탄절 (도치: how great are ~)

vocabulary.example: The company faced ethical questions about its AI products.
There are many dimensions to this problem.
She waited anxiously for the test results.

expressions.usage: 특정 시점 이후로 계속되는 상태를 표현할 때
강조구문(cleft sentence)으로 시점을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: It was only in [time] that [subject] [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 강조구문(cleft sentence). 특정 시점을 강조하여 "비로소 ~이 되어서야"라는 의미를 전달.
examples: It was only in 2020 that the world realized the threat of pandemics.
It was only after graduation that I understood the value of education.

drillQuestions.question: It _____ only in 1998 that I became aware of the dangers.
From the moment I became _____ in the creation of new technologies...

sentences.notes: 에세이의 첫 문장으로, 강조구문(It was ... that)과 도치(how great are the dangers)를 동시에 사용한 매우 복잡한 구조. "how great are the dangers"는 "how great the dangers are"의 도치로, 문어적/수사적 효과를 높인다.

koreanAlignment.en: From the moment I became involved in the creation of new technologies
koreanAlignment.ko: 새로운 기술의 창조에 관여하게 된 순간부터

koreanAlignment.en: their ethical dimensions have concerned me
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 윤리적 차원은 나를 걱정시켜 왔지만

koreanAlignment.en: but it was only in the autumn of 1998
of how great are the dangers facing us in the 21st century

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 강조구문(cleft sentence) + 도치(inversion) — 에세이 전체의 핵심 주제를 첫 문장에서 드라마틱하게 제시

sentences.original: I can date the onset of my unease to the day I met Ray Kurzweil, the deservedly famous inventor of the first reading machine for the blind and many other amazing things.
sentences.translation: 내 불안의 시작은 레이 커즈와일을 만난 날로 거슬러 올라간다. 그는 시각 장애인을 위한 최초의 독서 기계와 많은 다른 놀라운 것들을 만든, 명성을 받을 자격이 충분한 발명가이다.

grammarElements.label: 동사 (can + date: ~의 시기를 정할 수 있다)

grammarElements.text: to the day I met Ray Kurzweil
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (도달점)

grammarElements.text: the deservedly famous inventor of the first reading machine for the blind and many other amazing things
grammarElements.label: 동격어구 (Ray Kurzweil을 설명)

vocabulary.example: The onset of symptoms appeared within 24 hours.
A growing sense of unease filled the room.
She is deservedly proud of her achievements.

expressions.usage: 어떤 사건의 시작 시점을 특정할 때
질병, 감정, 현상 등이 처음 나타나는 시점을 표현

patterns.pattern: I can date [X] to the day [event]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 경험이나 감정의 시작을 특정 날짜/사건으로 소급하는 패턴. 회고적 서술에서 자주 사용.
examples: I can date my interest in music to the day I heard Beethoven.
She can date her career change to the moment she read that article.

drillQuestions.question: I can date the _____ of my unease to that day.
Ray Kurzweil is the _____ famous inventor of the reading machine.

sentences.notes: "date something to"는 "~의 시기를 ...로 정하다"라는 학술적 표현. 동격 구문(appositive)이 매우 길어, Ray Kurzweil에 대한 설명이 문장 끝까지 이어진다. deservedly는 "그럴 자격이 있는"이라는 필자의 평가를 담고 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: I can date the onset of my unease
to the day I met Ray Kurzweil
koreanAlignment.ko: 레이 커즈와일을 만난 날로 거슬러 올라간다

koreanAlignment.en: of the first reading machine for the blind
koreanAlignment.ko: 시각 장애인을 위한 최초의 독서 기계의

sentences.original: Ray and I were both speakers at George Gilder's Telecosm conference, and I encountered him by chance in the bar of the hotel after both our sessions were over.
sentences.translation: 레이와 나는 둘 다 조지 길더의 텔레코즘 컨퍼런스에서 연사였고, 우리 둘의 세션이 모두 끝난 후 호텔 바에서 우연히 그를 만났다.

grammarElements.text: both speakers at George Gilder's Telecosm conference
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: and I encountered him by chance
grammarElements.label: 등위절

grammarElements.text: in the bar of the hotel
grammarElements.label: 장소 전치사구

grammarElements.text: after both our sessions were over
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (시간 — after)

vocabulary.example: I encountered an old friend at the airport.
We met by chance at the bookstore.
The afternoon session focused on AI ethics.

expressions.usage: 계획하지 않은 만남이나 사건을 표현할 때
행사나 프로그램이 종료되었을 때

patterns.pattern: [A] and I were both [noun] at [event], and I [verb] [someone] by chance
patterns.explanation: 우연한 만남의 배경을 설명하는 서사 패턴. 장소와 상황을 구체적으로 설정.
examples: Tom and I were both volunteers at the charity event, and I ran into her by chance.
She and I were both guests at the wedding, and I encountered her by chance at the reception.

drillQuestions.question: I _____ him by chance in the bar of the hotel.
We met after both our sessions were _____.

sentences.notes: encounter는 meet보다 격식적이며 "우연한 만남"의 뉘앙스가 강하다. by chance가 이 우연성을 더욱 강조한다. 이 우연한 만남이 에세이 전체의 촉발점이 되므로 서사적으로 중요한 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: Ray and I were both speakers
and I encountered him by chance
in the bar of the hotel
after both our sessions were over
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리 둘의 세션이 모두 끝난 후

sentences.original: I was sitting with John Searle, a Berkeley philosopher who studies consciousness.
sentences.translation: 나는 의식을 연구하는 버클리의 철학자 존 설과 함께 앉아 있었다.

grammarElements.text: a Berkeley philosopher who studies consciousness
grammarElements.label: 동격어구 (John Searle을 설명) + 관계대명사절

vocabulary.example: Plato was one of the greatest philosophers in history.
The nature of consciousness is one of the deepest mysteries in science.

expressions.usage: 과거진행형으로 배경 상황을 묘사할 때

patterns.pattern: I was sitting with [person], a [title/description] who [verb]
patterns.explanation: 동격 구문으로 인물을 소개하며 배경을 설정하는 서사 패턴.
examples: I was talking with Dr. Kim, a neuroscientist who studies memory.
I was walking with Sarah, a journalist who covers technology.

drillQuestions.question: John Searle is a philosopher who studies _____.
I _____ sitting with John Searle at the bar.

sentences.notes: 과거진행형(was sitting)은 배경 상황을 묘사하며, 이어질 사건(Ray가 다가옴)의 무대를 설정한다. John Searle은 "중국어 방" 사고실험으로 유명한 실제 철학자. consciousness(의식)는 AI 논쟁의 핵심 개념.

koreanAlignment.en: I was sitting with John Searle
koreanAlignment.ko: 나는 존 설과 함께 앉아 있었다

sentences.original: While we were talking, Ray approached and a conversation began, the subject of which haunts me to this day.
sentences.translation: 우리가 이야기하는 동안, 레이가 다가왔고 대화가 시작되었는데, 그 주제는 오늘날까지 나를 괴롭힌다.

grammarElements.text: the subject of which haunts me to this day
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (of which — 계속적 용법)

vocabulary.example: A stranger approached me on the street.
The memory of the accident haunted her for years.

expressions.usage: 과거의 일이 현재까지 영향을 미치고 있음을 강조
관계대명사 계속적 용법으로 앞 문장 전체를 수식

patterns.pattern: [clause], the [noun] of which [verb] me to this day
patterns.explanation: 과거 사건의 현재적 영향을 강조하는 문어적 패턴. "of which"는 격식체 관계대명사.
examples: We had a meeting, the outcome of which surprises me to this day.
She made a promise, the significance of which I understood only years later.

drillQuestions.question: The subject of which _____ me to this day.
While we were talking, Ray _____ and a conversation began.

sentences.notes: "the subject of which"는 "전치사 + 관계대명사" 구조(= whose subject 또는 and its subject). 매우 문어적인 표현으로, 격식 있는 에세이에서 자주 사용된다. haunt는 유령(ghost)에서 파생된 동사로, 기억이나 생각이 "유령처럼 따라다닌다"는 비유적 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: the subject of which haunts me to this day

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 복선(foreshadowing) — "오늘날까지 나를 괴롭힌다"로 앞으로 전개될 심각한 논의를 암시

sentences.original: I had missed Ray's talk and the subsequent panel that Ray and John had been on, and they now picked right up where they'd left off, with Ray saying that the rate of improvement of technology was going to accelerate and that we were going to become robots or fuse with robots or something like that, and John countering that this couldn't happen, because the robots couldn't be conscious.
sentences.translation: 나는 레이의 강연과 레이와 존이 참여했던 후속 패널을 놓쳤는데, 그들은 이제 중단했던 곳에서 바로 이어갔다. 레이는 기술 향상의 속도가 가속될 것이며 우리가 로봇이 되거나 로봇과 융합하거나 그런 것이 될 것이라고 말했고, 존은 로봇이 의식을 가질 수 없기 때문에 그런 일은 일어날 수 없다고 반박했다.

grammarElements.text: I had missed Ray's talk and the subsequent panel
grammarElements.label: 주절 (과거완료 — 대과거)

grammarElements.text: that Ray and John had been on
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that)

grammarElements.text: they now picked right up where they'd left off
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (구동사 pick up)

vocabulary.example: The subsequent investigation revealed the truth.
The pace of technological change continues to accelerate.
The two companies fused into a single entity.
He countered her argument with solid evidence.

expressions.phrase: pick right up where they left off
expressions.usage: 이전에 하던 일이나 대화를 바로 재개할 때
정확한 표현이 아닌 대략적인 설명을 할 때

patterns.pattern: They picked right up where they'd left off, with [A] saying ... and [B] countering ...
patterns.explanation: "with + 주어 + 현재분사" 독립분사구문으로 두 사람의 대화를 동시에 묘사하는 패턴.
examples: They resumed the debate, with Tom arguing for regulation and Lisa countering with market solutions.

drillQuestions.question: They picked right up _____ they'd left off.
The rate of improvement of technology was going to _____.

sentences.notes: 에세이에서 가장 긴 문장 중 하나. "with + 주어 + 현재분사"(with Ray saying, John countering)는 독립분사구문으로, 동시에 일어나는 두 행위를 묘사한다. "pick up where one left off"는 관용구로 "이전에 중단한 곳에서 다시 시작하다".

koreanAlignment.en: I had missed Ray's talk and the subsequent panel
koreanAlignment.ko: 나는 레이의 강연과 후속 패널을 놓쳤는데

koreanAlignment.en: that Ray and John had been on
they now picked right up where they'd left off
with Ray saying that the rate of improvement of technology was going to accelerate
koreanAlignment.ko: 레이는 기술 향상의 속도가 가속될 것이라고 말했고

koreanAlignment.en: and that we were going to become robots or fuse with robots
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 로봇이 되거나 로봇과 융합할 것이라고

koreanAlignment.en: and John countering that this couldn't happen
koreanAlignment.ko: 존은 그런 일은 일어날 수 없다고 반박했다

koreanAlignment.en: because the robots couldn't be conscious
koreanAlignment.ko: 로봇이 의식을 가질 수 없기 때문에

sentences.original: While I had heard such talk before, I had always felt sentient robots were in the realm of science fiction.
sentences.translation: 그런 이야기를 전에도 들어본 적은 있었지만, 나는 항상 감각이 있는 로봇은 공상과학의 영역에 있다고 느꼈다.

grammarElements.text: While I had heard such talk before
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (while — ~이긴 하지만)

grammarElements.text: sentient robots were in the realm of science fiction
grammarElements.label: that절 (생략된 접속사 that)

vocabulary.example: Are machines capable of becoming sentient beings?
This discovery opens up a new realm of possibilities.

expressions.phrase: in the realm of science fiction
expressions.usage: 비현실적이거나 먼 미래의 것으로 여길 때
이전에 언급된 종류의 담론을 가리킬 때

patterns.pattern: While I had [past participle] before, I had always felt [clause]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 경험을 인정하면서도 그때까지의 견해가 달랐음을 대비하는 양보 패턴.
examples: While I had considered the idea before, I had always felt it was impractical.
While she had heard warnings before, she had always believed the risks were low.

drillQuestions.question: I had always felt sentient robots were in the _____ of science fiction.
_____ I had heard such talk before, I had always dismissed it.

sentences.notes: while은 여기서 "~하는 동안"이 아니라 "~이긴 하지만"이라는 양보의 의미. sentient는 AI 윤리에서 핵심 개념으로, "감각과 주관적 경험이 있는"을 의미한다. realm은 원래 "왕국"이라는 뜻에서 비유적으로 "영역"으로 확장.

koreanAlignment.en: While I had heard such talk before
koreanAlignment.ko: 그런 이야기를 전에도 들어본 적은 있었지만

koreanAlignment.en: sentient robots were in the realm of science fiction
koreanAlignment.ko: 감각이 있는 로봇은 공상과학의 영역에 있다고

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 양보(concession) — 이전 입장을 인정한 후 전환을 준비하는 수사적 장치

sentences.original: But now, from someone I respected, I was hearing a strong argument that they were a near-term possibility.
sentences.translation: 하지만 이제, 내가 존경하는 사람으로부터, 그것들이 가까운 미래에 실현 가능하다는 강력한 주장을 듣고 있었다.

grammarElements.text: that they were a near-term possibility
grammarElements.label: that절 (동격절 — argument의 내용)

vocabulary.example: The near-term outlook for the economy is uncertain.
She presented a strong argument in favor of the proposal.

expressions.usage: 정보의 출처에 대한 신뢰를 강조할 때
막연한 미래가 아닌 임박한 가능성을 표현

patterns.pattern: But now, from [source], I was hearing [noun] that [clause]
patterns.explanation: 이전 견해와의 극적 전환을 표현하는 패턴. "But now"로 시점 변화, 과거진행형으로 진행 중인 인식 변화를 묘사.
examples: But now, from a leading scientist, I was hearing evidence that climate change was irreversible.
But now, from my own students, I was seeing signs that the approach wasn't working.

drillQuestions.question: I was hearing that they were a _____-term possibility.
From someone I _____, I was hearing a strong argument.

sentences.notes: "But now"는 이전 문장(science fiction)과 극적인 대조를 이룬다. "someone I respected"에서 관계대명사(whom/that)가 생략되어 있다. 과거진행형(was hearing)은 깨달음이 순간이 아닌 과정임을 나타낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: I was hearing a strong argument
that they were a near-term possibility

sentences.original: I was taken aback, especially given Ray's proven ability to imagine and create the future.
sentences.translation: 나는 깜짝 놀랐다, 특히 미래를 상상하고 창조하는 레이의 입증된 능력을 감안하면 더욱 그러했다.

grammarElements.text: given Ray's proven ability to imagine and create the future
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (given — ~을 감안하면)

vocabulary.example: I was taken aback by her sudden resignation.
She has a proven track record in business.

expressions.usage: 예상치 못한 일에 충격받았을 때. be taken aback by ~
전제 조건이나 상황을 제시할 때 (= considering)

patterns.pattern: I was taken aback, especially given [someone's] [noun]
patterns.explanation: 놀라움의 감정을 표현한 후, "given"으로 그 이유를 보충하는 패턴.
examples: I was taken aback, especially given the company's reputation for quality.
We were taken aback, especially given the positive reviews we had read.

drillQuestions.question: I was taken _____, especially given Ray's ability.
_____ Ray's proven ability to create the future, I was shocked.

sentences.notes: "taken aback"은 항해 용어에서 유래한 관용구로, 돛이 갑자기 뒤로 밀리는 것에서 "충격을 받다"의 의미로 확장. "given"은 분사(과거분사)이지만 전치사처럼 기능하며 "~을 고려하면"이라는 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: to imagine and create the future

sentences.original: I already knew that new technologies like genetic engineering and nanotechnology were giving us the power to remake the world, but a realistic and imminent scenario for intelligent robots surprised me.
sentences.translation: 유전공학과 나노기술 같은 새로운 기술들이 세상을 다시 만들 수 있는 힘을 우리에게 준다는 것은 이미 알고 있었지만, 지능형 로봇에 대한 현실적이고 임박한 시나리오는 나를 놀라게 했다.

grammarElements.text: that new technologies like genetic engineering and nanotechnology were giving us the power to remake the world
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: a realistic and imminent scenario for intelligent robots
grammarElements.label: 주어

vocabulary.example: Genetic engineering has revolutionized agriculture.
Nanotechnology operates at the molecular level.
An imminent threat required immediate action.
Technology has the power to remake entire industries.

expressions.phrase: the power to remake the world
expressions.usage: 혁신적 변화의 잠재력을 표현할 때
추상적이 아닌 구체적이고 가까운 가능성을 강조

patterns.pattern: I already knew that [X], but [Y] surprised me
patterns.explanation: 기존 지식을 인정하면서도 새로운 충격을 대비하는 양보-전환 패턴.
examples: I already knew that AI was advancing, but the speed of progress surprised me.
We already knew the risks existed, but the scale of the damage surprised us.

drillQuestions.question: A realistic and _____ scenario for intelligent robots surprised me.
New technologies were giving us the power to _____ the world.

sentences.notes: imminent(임박한)과 eminent(저명한)은 자주 혼동되는 단어. 이 문장은 Joy가 기존에 인식하고 있던 위험(유전공학, 나노기술)과 새로운 충격(지능형 로봇)을 대비시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: new technologies like genetic engineering and nanotechnology
were giving us the power to remake the world
koreanAlignment.ko: 세상을 다시 만들 힘을 우리에게 준다는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: but a realistic and imminent scenario

koreanAlignment.ko: 지능형 로봇에 대한, 나를 놀라게 했다

sentences.original: It's easy to get jaded about such breakthroughs.
sentences.translation: 그러한 획기적인 발견에 시큰둥해지기는 쉽다.

grammarElements.text: to get jaded about such breakthroughs
grammarElements.label: 진주어 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: Years of repetitive work left him jaded.
The scientist achieved a major breakthrough in cancer research.

expressions.usage: 너무 많이 접해서 더 이상 놀라지 않게 될 때

patterns.pattern: It's easy to get [adjective] about [noun]
patterns.explanation: 가주어 it 구문으로, 특정 감정이나 태도에 빠지기 쉬움을 표현.
examples: It's easy to get complacent about security.
It's easy to get cynical about politics.

drillQuestions.question: It's easy to get _____ about such breakthroughs.
It's easy _____ get complacent about success.

sentences.notes: jaded는 원래 "지친 말"(jade = 늙은 말)에서 파생된 형용사로, "너무 많이 경험해서 흥미를 잃은" 상태를 뜻한다. 짧은 문장으로 독자의 공감을 유도하는 효과가 있다.

sentences.original: We hear in the news almost every day of some kind of technological or scientific advance.
sentences.translation: 우리는 거의 매일 뉴스에서 어떤 종류의 기술적 또는 과학적 진보에 대해 듣는다.

grammarElements.text: of some kind of technological or scientific advance
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (내용 — hear of)

vocabulary.example: Medical advances have extended human life expectancy.
Technological innovation drives economic growth.

expressions.usage: 매체를 통해 정보를 접할 때. hear of는 "~에 대해 듣다"
불특정한 유형을 가리킬 때

patterns.pattern: We hear [in the media] almost every day of [noun]
patterns.explanation: 일상적으로 접하는 정보의 빈도를 표현하는 패턴. "hear of"는 "~에 대해 듣다".
examples: We read in the papers almost every week of some new data breach.
We see on social media almost daily some kind of political controversy.

drillQuestions.question: We hear in the news almost every day _____ some kind of advance.
Some kind of technological or _____ advance.

sentences.notes: "hear of"에서 in the news와 almost every day가 삽입되어 "hear ... of"가 분리되어 있다. 이를 "hear of"로 다시 연결하면 구조가 명확해진다.

koreanAlignment.en: of some kind of technological or scientific advance
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 종류의 기술적 또는 과학적 진보에 대해

sentences.original: Yet this was no ordinary prediction.
sentences.translation: 그러나 이것은 보통의 예측이 아니었다.

vocabulary.example: This was no ordinary day—it was her birthday.
His prediction about the election proved correct.

expressions.usage: "no + 형용사" 구조로 강한 부정을 표현. "not an ordinary"보다 더 강조적

patterns.pattern: Yet this was no ordinary [noun].
patterns.explanation: 짧고 강렬한 전환 문장. "no ordinary"로 예외성을 강조하여 독자의 주의를 환기.
examples: Yet this was no ordinary crisis.
Yet this was no ordinary meeting.

drillQuestions.question: _____ this was no ordinary prediction.
This was _____ ordinary prediction.

sentences.notes: "no ordinary"는 "not an ordinary"보다 훨씬 강한 부정으로, 문학적 효과가 크다. 의도적으로 짧은 문장을 배치하여 앞의 긴 문장들과 리듬적 대비를 만들고, 독자의 주의를 집중시킨다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 짧은 전환문(short pivot sentence) — 긴 문장 뒤에 의도적으로 배치된 짧은 문장으로 독자의 주의 환기

sentences.original: In the hotel bar, Ray gave me a partial preprint of his then-forthcoming book The Age of Spiritual Machines, which outlined a utopia he foresaw—one in which humans gained near immortality by becoming one with robotic technology.
sentences.translation: 호텔 바에서, 레이는 당시 출간 예정이었던 그의 책 "영적 기계의 시대"의 부분 사전 인쇄본을 나에게 주었는데, 그 책은 그가 예견한 유토피아를 개괄했다—인간이 로봇 기술과 하나가 됨으로써 거의 불멸을 얻는 유토피아였다.

grammarElements.text: a partial preprint of his then-forthcoming book The Age of Spiritual Machines
grammarElements.label: 직접목적어

grammarElements.text: which outlined a utopia he foresaw
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (계속적 용법)

grammarElements.text: one in which humans gained near immortality by becoming one with robotic technology
grammarElements.label: 동격어구 (utopia를 재정의)

vocabulary.example: The researcher shared a preprint of the study before peer review.
Details will be revealed in her forthcoming book.
The novel describes a technological utopia.
The quest for immortality has fascinated humans throughout history.

expressions.usage: 과거 시점에서 미래를 가리키는 합성 형용사. then은 "당시"의 의미
두 존재가 완전히 합쳐짐을 표현

patterns.pattern: [book title], which outlined [a vision]—one in which [description]
patterns.explanation: 책의 핵심 비전을 소개한 후, 대시(—)와 동격으로 그 비전을 상세히 설명하는 학술적 패턴.
examples: The report outlined a future—one in which renewable energy replaced all fossil fuels.
The manifesto described a society—one in which wealth was equally distributed.

drillQuestions.question: The book outlined a _____ he foresaw.
Humans gained near _____ by becoming one with robotic technology.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 매우 긴 복합 구조. "then-forthcoming"은 하이픈으로 연결된 합성 형용사로 "당시 곧 출간될"이라는 의미. 대시(—)로 시작되는 동격 구문이 utopia의 내용을 구체적으로 설명한다. "near immortality"는 "거의 불멸"로, 완전한 불멸이 아닌 점에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: Ray gave me a partial preprint
koreanAlignment.ko: 레이는 부분 사전 인쇄본을 나에게 주었다

koreanAlignment.en: of his then-forthcoming book The Age of Spiritual Machines
koreanAlignment.ko: 당시 출간 예정이었던 그의 책 "영적 기계의 시대"의

koreanAlignment.en: which outlined a utopia he foresaw
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 책은 그가 예견한 유토피아를 개괄했다

koreanAlignment.en: one in which humans gained near immortality
by becoming one with robotic technology

sentences.original: On reading it, my sense of unease only intensified; I felt sure he had to be understating the dangers, understating the probability of a bad outcome along this path.
sentences.translation: 그것을 읽자, 내 불안감은 오히려 심화되었다. 그가 위험을 축소하고 있다고, 이 경로에서 나쁜 결과가 나올 확률을 축소하고 있다고 확신했다.

grammarElements.text: he had to be understating the dangers
grammarElements.label: that절 생략 (felt의 목적어)

grammarElements.text: understating the probability of a bad outcome along this path
grammarElements.label: 병렬 분사구문

vocabulary.example: The debate only intensified after the report was published.
The report understated the severity of the problem.
The probability of success is high.

expressions.usage: "on + 동명사"는 격식체에서 "~하자마자"의 의미
"only"가 "예상과 달리 더"라는 의미를 강조
"have to be + 현재분사"로 강한 추측을 표현

patterns.pattern: On [verb]-ing it, [emotion/reaction] only intensified
patterns.explanation: "on + 동명사" 구문으로 행동과 그 즉각적 반응을 연결하는 문어적 패턴.
examples: On hearing the news, her anxiety only intensified.
On examining the data, our concerns only deepened.

drillQuestions.question: _____ reading it, my sense of unease intensified.
He had to be _____ the dangers.

sentences.notes: "on + 동명사"는 매우 격식적인 구문으로 "~하자마자"의 의미. "only intensified"에서 only는 "단지"가 아니라 "오히려, 더욱"이라는 강조 부사. "had to be understating"은 must be understating과 같은 강한 추측으로, "축소하고 있는 것이 틀림없다"의 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: my sense of unease only intensified
he had to be understating the dangers
understating the probability of a bad outcome
koreanAlignment.ko: 나쁜 결과가 나올 확률을 축소하고 있다고

sentences.original: I found myself most troubled by a passage detailing a dystopian scenario:
sentences.translation: 나는 디스토피아적 시나리오를 상세히 기술한 한 구절에 가장 마음이 어지러워진 나 자신을 발견했다:

grammarElements.text: by a passage detailing a dystopian scenario
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (원인) + 현재분사구

vocabulary.example: The novel paints a dystopian picture of the future.
This passage from the book is particularly moving.
The report detailed the findings of the investigation.

expressions.usage: "find oneself + 과거분사"로 스스로 인식하지 못한 감정 상태를 발견할 때
최상급 부사 most + 형용사로 감정의 강도를 표현

patterns.pattern: I found myself [past participle] by [noun]
patterns.explanation: "find oneself + 과거분사"는 무의식적으로 특정 상태에 놓여 있음을 발견하는 문학적 표현.
examples: I found myself moved by the documentary.
She found herself captivated by the speaker's words.

drillQuestions.question: I found _____ most troubled by the passage.
A passage detailing a _____ scenario.

sentences.notes: "find oneself + 과거분사"는 자기 자신도 예상치 못한 감정 상태를 발견한다는 문학적 표현. dystopian은 utopia의 반대인 dystopia에서 파생된 형용사로, "이상향의 반대, 암울한 미래사회"를 뜻한다.

sentences.original: In the book, you don't discover until you turn the page that the author of this passage is Theodore Kaczynski—the Unabomber.
sentences.translation: 그 책에서, 당신은 페이지를 넘기기 전까지는 이 구절의 저자가 시어도어 카진스키—유나바머—라는 것을 알지 못한다.

grammarElements.text: that the author of this passage is Theodore Kaczynski—the Unabomber
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적어) + 동격 대시

vocabulary.example: Scientists discovered a new species in the Amazon.
The author of the passage remained anonymous.

expressions.usage: "not ... until" 구문으로 특정 시점까지의 무지를 강조

patterns.pattern: You don't [verb] until [event] that [revelation]
patterns.explanation: "not ... until" 구문에 that절이 결합된 서스펜스 패턴. 극적 반전을 연출.
examples: You don't realize until you read the footnote that the data was fabricated.
You don't understand until the end that the narrator is unreliable.

drillQuestions.question: You don't discover _____ you turn the page that the author is Kaczynski.
The _____ of this passage is Theodore Kaczynski.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 에세이에서 가장 극적인 반전의 순간. "not ... until" 구문으로 서스펜스를 만들고, 대시(—)로 "the Unabomber"라는 충격적 정체를 밝힌다. Theodore Kaczynski(유나바머)는 1978-1995년 폭탄 테러를 저질러 3명을 살해한 수학자.

koreanAlignment.en: that the author of this passage is Theodore Kaczynski
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 구절의 저자가 시어도어 카진스키라는 것을

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 극적 반전(dramatic reveal) — "not ... until" 서스펜스 구조와 대시를 활용한 충격적 정체 공개

sentences.original: I am no apologist for Kaczynski.
sentences.translation: 나는 카진스키를 옹호하는 사람이 결코 아니다.

vocabulary.example: He is not an apologist for violence.

expressions.usage: 특정 인물이나 사상에 대한 지지를 강하게 부정할 때. 논쟁적 주제를 다루기 전 면책의 의미

patterns.pattern: I am no [noun] for [someone/something]
patterns.explanation: "no + 명사" 강한 부정 패턴. "not an X"보다 더 단호한 거부를 표현.
examples: I am no expert in this field.
I am no supporter of extremism.

drillQuestions.question: I am _____ apologist for Kaczynski.

sentences.notes: "no + 명사"는 "not a/an + 명사"보다 더 강한 부정. 이 짧은 문장은 다음에 이어질 Kaczynski 논의에 대한 면책(disclaimer) 역할. Joy는 테러리스트의 주장에 일부 동의하면서도 먼저 자신의 입장을 분명히 한다.

sentences.original: Kaczynski's actions were murderous and, in my view, criminally insane.
sentences.translation: 카진스키의 행동은 살인적이었고, 내 관점에서, 범죄적으로 미친 것이었다.

grammarElements.text: and, in my view, criminally insane
grammarElements.label: 병렬 보어 (삽입구 포함)

vocabulary.example: The dictator launched a murderous campaign against dissidents.
He was found criminally negligent.
The court ruled the defendant legally insane.

expressions.usage: 개인적 의견을 삽입할 때. 문장 중간에 콤마로 삽입
법률 용어에서 유래한 표현으로 극단적 비정상을 나타냄

patterns.pattern: [Someone's] actions were [adjective A] and, in my view, [adjective B]
patterns.explanation: 행위에 대한 판단을 두 형용사로 표현하며, 삽입구로 개인 의견임을 명시.
examples: The company's decisions were reckless and, in my view, irresponsible.
His comments were offensive and, in my view, completely inappropriate.

drillQuestions.question: Kaczynski's actions were _____ and criminally insane.
_____ my view, his actions were insane.

sentences.notes: "in my view"는 삽입구(parenthetical)로, 이것이 객관적 사실이 아닌 개인적 판단임을 밝힌다. Joy는 다음 문장에서 Kaczynski의 주장에 일부 동의할 것이므로, 먼저 그의 행동을 강력히 비판하는 것이 수사적으로 중요하다.

sentences.original: He is clearly a Luddite, but simply saying this does not dismiss his argument; as difficult as it is for me to acknowledge, I saw some merit in the reasoning in this single passage.
sentences.translation: 그는 분명 러다이트이지만, 그렇게 말하는 것만으로는 그의 주장을 일축할 수 없다. 인정하기 어렵지만, 나는 이 단일 구절의 추론에서 일부 가치를 보았다.

grammarElements.text: but simply saying this does not dismiss his argument
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (대조)

grammarElements.text: as difficult as it is for me to acknowledge
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (as ... as — 아무리 ~하더라도)

grammarElements.text: in the reasoning in this single passage
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (장소/대상)

vocabulary.example: He's often called a Luddite for his distrust of technology.
You shouldn't dismiss her concerns so easily.
The proposal has some merit.
She acknowledged her mistake publicly.

expressions.usage: "as + 형용사 + as + 주어 + be"로 양보를 표현하는 격식체

expressions.phrase: simply saying this does not dismiss
expressions.meaning: 단지 이렇게 말하는 것으로는 일축할 수 없다
expressions.usage: 표면적 반박이 불충분함을 지적할 때
논적의 주장에도 일리가 있음을 조심스럽게 인정

patterns.pattern: [X] is clearly a [label], but simply saying this does not dismiss [their argument]
patterns.explanation: 상대를 규정하되, 그 규정만으로 논박이 되지 않음을 인정하는 지적 정직성의 패턴.
examples: He is clearly biased, but simply saying this does not dismiss his data.
The theory is clearly controversial, but simply labeling it so does not refute it.

drillQuestions.question: Simply saying this does not _____ his argument.
As difficult as it is for me to _____, I saw some merit.

sentences.notes: Luddite는 19세기 영국에서 기계 파괴 운동을 벌인 노동자들에서 유래한 단어로, 현대에는 "기술 발전을 반대하는 사람"을 지칭. "as difficult as it is"는 양보의 부사절로, 필자의 내적 갈등을 솔직하게 드러낸다. 이 문장은 에세이의 핵심 긴장—테러리스트의 논리에서 가치를 발견하는 지적 정직성—을 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: but simply saying this does not dismiss his argument
koreanAlignment.ko: 그렇게 말하는 것만으로는 그의 주장을 일축할 수 없다

koreanAlignment.en: as difficult as it is for me to acknowledge
in the reasoning in this single passage

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 지적 정직성(intellectual honesty) — 적대자의 주장에서도 가치를 인정하는 용기를 보여줌으로써 필자의 신뢰성을 높임
